data.title: Qatar Embassy donates $1 million to Wounded Warrior Project for adaptive sports in Florida, supporting veterans.
data.description: In Jacksonville, Florida on January 29, 2026, the Wounded Warrior Project® (WWP) was granted a substantial $1 million donation from the Embassy of the State of Qatar. The aim of this contribution is to support adaptive sports programs for warriors in Florida. This generous funding will enable veterans to engage in various sports activities, enhancing their physical fitness and overall well-being.

The European Commission has registered a civil initiative for a complete ban on all imports from Russia and Belarus into the European Union. The authors of the initiative need to collect at least one million signatures in support of it in at least seven EU countries.

data.title: CVR Energy to offer $1 billion in senior notes due 2031 and 2034 in private placement. Business Wire, Sugar Land, Texas.
data.description: In Sugar Land, Texas, CVR Energy, Inc. (NYSE: CVI) disclosed plans to conduct a private offering under Rule 144A and Regulation S of the Securities Act of 1933. The offering, contingent on market conditions, will involve $1 billion in combined principal amount. This will include senior unsecured notes due in 2031 (referred to as the "2031 Notes") and senior unsecured notes due in 2034 (referred to as the "2034 Notes").

data.title: Oracle's OPERA Cloud chosen by IHG Hotels & Resorts to improve property management across Americas & EMEAA regions.
data.description: In a recent development, Oracle will implement its OPERA Cloud hospitality platform to boost operational efficiency and elevate guest experiences across InterContinental Hotels Group (IHG) properties throughout the Americas, Europe, Middle East, Africa, and Asia (EMEAA) regions. This decision was made official in Austin, Texas on January 29, 2026. The Oracle OPERA Cloud platform aims to streamline processes and enhance customer service at numerous IHG locations across these key regions.

data.title: American Arbitration Association marks 100 years of leading dispute resolution innovation since it was founded in 1926.
data.description: 100 years have passed since the founding of the American Arbitration Association in 1926, marking a significant milestone in the evolution of dispute resolution. As a leader in this field, the association has made a lasting impact over the last century by shaping the future of resolving conflicts.
